data.title: The waiting is almost over for Tesla's watershed robotaxi event
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) closed down 1% on Thursday as traders prepped for the "We, Robot" event at 10:00 p.m. The title is a reference to Isaac Asimov’s science fiction short stories called "I, Robot." CEO Elon Musk is expected to unveil the design for a dedicated robotaxi or Cybercab, update on the FSD software package, highlight the Optimus humanoid robot, and show off either prototypes or previews of new models, potentially including the long-awaited $25K EV, an autonomous truck, or a Cybervan.

Some analysts see the event as a watershed test of whether Tesla (TSLA) deserves its premium valuation as a tech disruptor. Musk has been talking up the potential for autonomous driving for years. "The value of a fully electric autonomous fleet is generally gigantic — boggles the mind, really," he stated back in 2021. "That will be one of the most valuable things that's ever done in the history of civilization," he added. More recently, he said that Tesla (TSLA) is not a car company, but should be thought of as an AI robotics company.

Seeking Alpha Investing Group Leader Victor Dergunov thinks the introduction of a $25,000 compact EV and robotaxi fleet could significantly boost Tesla's (TSLA) market share and profitability, driving future stock price appreciation.

data.title: Upstart to sell $2B of consumer loans to Blue Owl Capital
data.description:
Upstart Holdings (NASDAQ:UPST) said Thursday it has agreed to sell $2B of its consumer installment loans to private credit lender Blue Owl Capital (NYSE:OWL).

The transaction will be made over 18 months, and includes an initial purchase of a $290M personal loan portfolio, which closed in September.

The deal was structured and closed by alternative credit manager Atalaya Capital Management. Blue Owl (OWL) completed its acquisition of Atalaya on Sept. 30, representing an expansion of Blue Owl’s alternative credit presence and supplementing its market-leading position in direct lending.

ATLAS SP Partners, a warehouse finance and securitized products business, will provide the debt financing for the loan purchases.

In midafternoon trading, (UPST) edged up 0.4%, while (OWL) gained 1.4%.

data.title: Triumph Group jumps after report it's exploring a sale
data.description:
Triumph Group (NYSE:TGI) soared 19% after a report that the aircraft components maker, is exploring its options, including a potential sale.

Triumph (TGI) is working with an adviser to solicit interest from strategic and private equity shops, according to a Bloomberg report on Thursday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

Deliberations are at an early stage and Triumph (TGI) may decide not to sell itself, according to the report. Triumph has a market value of $900 million.

Developing story ...

data.title: TD Bank to pay over $3B, subject to asset cap in U.S. AML settlement
data.description:
TD Bank Group (NYSE:TD) stock slid 5.4% in Thursday afternoon trading after bank agreed to pay more than $3B to settle investigations into its anti-money laundering ("AML") practices.

The company and some of its U.S. subsidiaries entered consent orders with the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency, the Federal Reserve Board, and the Financial Crimes Enforcment Network (FincEN) and entered into plea agreements with the U.S. Department of Justice and the U.S. Attorney's Office for the District of New Jersey.

It will pay a total of ~US$3.09B, which is largely covered by previous provisions of US$3.05B. An asset cap of US$434B was placed on the total assets of TD's two U.S. banking subsidiaries — TD Bank, NA, and TD Bank USA, NA.

The settlement also included requirements to remediate its U.S. AML program, that are broadly aligned with its existing remediation program; a requirement to prioritize the funding and staffing of the remediation; formal oversight of the AML remediation through a monitor; and a requirement for more stringent approval processes for new bank products, services, markets and stores.

"We have taken full responsibility for the failures of our U.S. AML program and are making the investments, changes and enhancements required to deliver on our commitment," said CEO and Group President Bharat Masran.

The Wall Street Journal had previously reported the expected terms of the deal.

data.title: Will Pfizer transform in response to Starboard’s activism?
data.description:
Hedge fund Starboard Value’s latest activist campaign targeting Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) has boosted the company’s shares in recent days even as Wall Street remains divided over its prospects.

Early this week, the Wall Street Journal and CNBC reported that Starboard wanted value-creating measures at Pfizer (PFE) after taking approximately a $1B stake in the struggling COVID-19 vaccine maker.

The move, backed by former Pfizer (PFE) executives Ian Read and Frank D'Amelio, sent the company's stock to a two-month high on Thursday with PFE CEO Albert Bourla agreeing to meet with the activist investor next week.

Since the report, Pfizer (PFE) has outperformed its big pharma rivals until Thursday when Read, who led PFE from 2010 to 2018 as CEO, and D'Amelio, who was the company's CFO from 2007 to 2021, dropped out of the campaign.

Seeking Alpha analyst ONeil Trader is among the skeptics of Starboard's move, noting in a recent article that the New York-based investment adviser has a checkered past when it comes to biopharma activist campaigns.

"Additional cost cutting, more share repurchases, or management and board changes are unlikely to make a change for the better at Pfizer," the analyst wrote, adding that the management has already taken those measures.

Wells Fargo analyst Mohit Bansal agreed. "We remain skeptical on what an activist investor could bring to the table," Bansal wrote with an equal weight rating and a $30 target on Pfizer (PFE).

"In many cases, these involvements spur cost management, but PFE is already doing that. PFE's issue is a top-line challenge where execution is key," Bansal added.

BMO Capital Markets analyst Evan David Seigerman offered similar views, arguing that while Pfizer (PFE) was the ideal target for an activist campaign, its turnaround story, similar to Eli Lilly's (LLY), could take years.

"We understand why an activist might think that Pfizer is the right story to get involved in, but management appears to actually be undertaking many of the corrective actions to right the ship," the analyst added with an Outperform rating and a $36 target on the stock.

However, Seeking Alpha analyst Hawkinvest sees value in Starboard's involvement. "I think they see a lot of value in this stock, and Starboard's involvement could become a catalyst to unlock value," Hawkinvest wrote.

data.title: SEC charges crypto firm Cumberland DRW for operating as an unregistered dealer
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on Thursday filed a lawsuit against Cumberland DRW, accusing the crypto market maker of operating as an unregistered dealer in over $2B of crypto assets offered and sold as securities.

The regulator alleged that since at least March 2018 through the present, Cumberland has acted as an unregistered dealer by buying and selling crypto assets offered and sold as securities for its own accounts as part of its regular business.

The complaint, filed in District Court for the Northern District of Illinois, further alleged that Cumberland engages in trading crypto assets that are offered and sold as investment contracts on third-party crypto asset exchanges as part of its regular business.

"Cumberland profited from its dealer activity in these assets without providing investors and the market with the important protections afforded by registration," said Jorge G. Tenreiro, acting chief of the SEC’s Crypto Assets and Cyber Unit.

The SEC's complaint seeks permanent injunctive relief, as well as disgorgement of ill-gotten gains, prejudgment interest and civil penalties.

data.title: Why is Wall Street shrugging off geopolitical risk and powering to new highs?
data.description:
Nine days ago, Iran launched about 200 missiles at Israel in retaliation for the killing of Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah, sparking widespread concerns over escalation in the Middle East. Oil prices soared in response.

Perhaps more surprisingly, risk assets and equities in particular have largely shrugged off the geopolitical worries. Wall Street's benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) on Wednesday notched its 44th record close of 2024, with 56 trading days still to go.

So what's up? Why are markets appearing so averse to geopolitical risk?

"There's no doubt that there are geopolitical jitters, but it seems artificial intelligence (AI) and mega-cap tech are stealing the show. While we won't get earnings reports from the Magnificent Seven stocks until later this month and into November, maybe the first-half winners are gearing up for a year-end rally," Seeking Alpha analyst Mike Zaccardi said.

"Unease in the Middle East has brought upside to oil and even inflation expectations, but it doesn't appear that tensions have escalated from last weekend," Zaccardi added.

Another aspect to consider is the fact that investors are seemingly confident that the Middle East situation will not have any major effect on the economy as a whole. That confidence has strengthened in recent days amid easing fears that Israel would launch a direct attack on Iran's oil assets, in a region that is home to roughly a third of the world’s energy inventory.

"Today’s geopolitical risks aren’t having a substantial impact on global growth or macro data. Indeed, the biggest market moves recently have been in response to China’s stimulus and the U.S. jobs report, precisely because they shifted the macro trajectory relative to previous expectations," Deutsche Bank macro strategist Henry Allen said in a research note on Monday.

"The global inflationary risks from the Middle East have remained manageable for the time being, with Brent crude oil prices still below their 2024 average. That is unlike what happened in 2022 after Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, when energy prices surged to their highest levels in many years," Allen added.

Keep in mind that market participants have developed a thick skin towards geopolitical risk for a while now, since Russia's invasion of Ukraine in 2022 and amid last year's conflict between Hamas and Israel. Through it all, stocks have continued to march higher.

"One of the few eternal truths for markets is there's always uncertainty about the future, as it's inherently unknowable. Risk scenarios can happen at any time, whether that's geopolitics or another unexpected event like a pandemic," Deutsche Bank's Allen said.

"Clearly, there are times when those risks are more elevated than others, and right now there are several points of geopolitical tension. However, there has never been a time when geopolitical risk isn't present. After all, in the decades of the Cold War there were regularly major tensions between the US and the Soviet Union. So this is always something that markets are grappling with to some extent," the strategist added.

On Thursday, oil prices advanced after Hurricane Milton's landfall in Florida and as traders awaited Israel's possible military response to Iran. WTI crude oil futures (CL1:COM) were last up 3.7% to $75.91/barrel in afternoon trade. Brent crude futures (CO1:COM) - the international oil benchmark - also rose 3.7% to $79.44/barrel.

Meanwhile, Wall Street fluctuated after receiving a hotter-than-anticipated consumer inflation report. The S&P 500 (SP500) was hovering about 25 points under its record intraday high, last -0.3%.

Here are some exchange-traded funds of interest tied to the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500), oil and energy: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (USO), (UCO), (DBO), (OILK), (USL), (XLE), (AMLP), (VDE), (XOP), (OIH), and (IXC).

data.title: High dividend growth + high free cash flow yield stocks = a winning strategy
data.description:
Stocks with high dividend growth and high free cash flow yield can generate more returns than stocks seeing high dividend growth alone, according to a Wolfe Research note.

This defensive strategy has historically outperformed the S&P 500 (SP500) by 500 basis points annually, said Chris Senyek, chief investment strategist.

data.title: A look at real estate stocks after housing inflation moderates, mortgages rise
data.description:
In September's CPI report, the pace of shelter cost increases moderated, slowing to +0.2% from +0.5% in August and +0.4% in July. In other words, housing prices are still rising, but at a slower rate than in the previous two months.

In addition, mortgage rates are climbing. The average rate on a 30-year fixed mortgage rose to 6.32% in the week ended Oct. 10, up from 1.62% in the prior week, its largest one-week jump in almost six months, according to Freddie Mac.

As a result, home affordability remains challenging. The cooling in housing costs is being offset by the increase in mortgage rates. Furthermore, initial jobless claims jumped in the past week, which is not a good sign for the housing market. People without jobs can't afford to buy houses or pay higher rent.

Real estate stocks are leading the broader stock averages lower in Thursday afternoon trading. The Real Estate Sector SPDR ETF (NYSEARCA:XLRE) is falling -1.1%, compared with the S&P 500's -0.4% decline. The iShares U.S. Home Construction ETF (BATS:ITB) slid 1.5%.

Real estate brokers' stocks led the drop. Anywhere Real Estate (NYSE:HOUS) slid 3.0%, Redfin (NASDAQ:RDFN) sank 3.3%, Opendoor Technologies (NASDAQ:OPEN), known for flipping houses, fell 5.0%, and Compass (NYSE:COMP) is down 1.2%. Zillow (NASDAQ:Z) class C shares, though, rose 0.2%.

Homebuilders also felt the pain. D.R. Horton (DHI) stock slipped 1.1%, KB Home (KBH) fell 1.5%, PulteGroup (PHM) dipped 0.6%, Lennar (LEN) -1.2%, Toll Brothers (TOL) -1.8%, and Beazer Homes USA (BZH) -1.4%.

Most home rental REITs climbed during the session. Invitation Homes (INVH) increased 0.4%, AMH (AMH), formerly American Homes 4 Rent,+0.2%, Bluerock Homes Trust (BHM) -0.2%, and NexPoint Diversified Real Estate (NXDT), +2.3%.

Meanwhile, apartment REITs slipped. Equity Residential (EQR) declined 1.0%, Independence Realty Trust (IRT), -1.3%, AvalonBay Communities (AVB) -0.9%, and Camden Property Trust (CPT) -1.5%.

Mortgage lenders were mixed. Rocket Companies (RKT) +0.9%, UWM Holdings (UWMC) -2.2%, and PennyMac Financial Services (PFSI) -0.8%.
